title: Connect workspace or folder to dashboard
excerpt: I am setting up calendar and workload widgets on a dashboard to monitor team work, and it’s pretty cumbersome to have to connect and filter every individual board to the dashboard. We will have dozens of boards when we’r…

posts.author: ashleyc7 (Ashley Crane)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am setting up calendar and workload widgets on a dashboard to monitor team work, and it’s pretty cumbersome to have to connect and filter every individual board to the dashboard. We will have dozens of boards when we’re fully set up in Monday. It would be great to be able to just connect to a workspace instead, so we know we’re capturing every board in that workspace.

It’s also a tiring experience to have to find boards to add, since I can’t just scroll through all boards available and can only search to add.

posts.author: tiger (Jay)
posts.cookedHtml:
Agreed, this has been an issue for a while now. Let me know if you find a workaround.

I requested this feature in 2019… still waiting…

Create a group of boards for dashboards Feature requests
I have several widgets on my dashboards. Each one requires me to go in and select boards one-by-one. If I add a new board, I have to manually add it to all the widgets. Is there a way to create a group of boards that can be referenced, similar to the teams feature for grouping people? It would work like this: Create a group of boards called “boardgroup” In the dashboard widgets, choose “boardgroup” rather than selecting each board. This will aggregate content from all the boards into one. Wh…
